statement: Use source-first enrichment: confirm a trusted source and exact part-number match before extracting facts; unresolved cases should fail closed to review instead of generating fake specificity.
notes: Captured from PEHA naming recovery and broader enrichment cleanup; exact PN match takes precedence over title wording; fact extraction starts only after trusted-source confirmation.

statement: Preferred source order is official manufacturer page or PDF, then large B2B distributor with exact PN, then technical B2B catalog with exact match; local repo artifacts are hints only, while marketplace, SEO, and auction pages are weak hints only.
notes: Trust order derived from successful SKU resolution work; repo artifacts help triage but must not be treated as truth; weak pages can supply hints but must not directly drive exact attributes or canonical titles.

statement: PEHA naming must be family-aware: generic titles such as 'Электроустановочный модуль PEHA ...' are unsafe; confirmed rules are 20.671/20.672/20.673/20.674/20.675 as NOVA frame with gang counts 1/2/3/4/5, 20.57 as AURA, 95.57 as DIALOG frame, 95.6511 as DIALOG socket, and TV/UAE/USB/TAE/RADIO/MP3 tokens usually indicate insert.
notes: Weak hints must not produce exact attributes; repo artifacts are hints only; family and product type should be resolved before title assembly; insert-token heuristic is useful but should still fail closed when family or exact subtype remains unclear.

statement: New-brand naming methodology: 1-2 anchor SKU with strong repo clues, then pattern discovery in source_name/family coding, then cluster expansion, then external lookup only for unknown clusters, then individual lookup only for edge cases; patterns belong in naming_families/*.json while source memory is stored separately.
notes: Temporary bootstrap memory artifact from the PEHA naming/research batch; this is a reusable process rule, not a runtime hook.

statement: SKU 106511, 127411, and 155111 are confirmed as PEHA AURA socket items.
notes: Cluster confirmed across target batch: 106511 / 127411 / 155111 -> AURA / socket.

statement: SKU 174813 is confirmed as PEHA AURA insert.
notes: Exact PN/product confirmation on e-partner PDF; treated as insert-class accessory.

statement: SKU 179951 is confirmed as PEHA NOVA insert.
notes: Confirmed via exact AudioPoint PDF mirror with exact article mapping; usable because PN/product confirmation is exact.

statement: SKU 22811, 37011, and 52811 are confirmed as PEHA NOVA rocker items.
notes: Cluster confirmed across target batch: 22811 / 37011 / 52811 -> NOVA / rocker.

statement: SKU 29313 and 940293 are confirmed as PEHA NOVA decorative elements.
notes: 940293 is confirmed by exact product page; 29313 is confirmed by exact catalog/PN mapping from Elektrobode. Both normalize to decorative_element.

statement: SKU 309811, 821811, 830811, and 887111 are confirmed as PEHA DIALOG rocker items.
notes: Cluster confirmed across target batch: 309811 / 821811 / 830811 / 887111 -> DIALOG / rocker.

statement: SKU 603111 is confirmed as PEHA COMPACTA frame.
notes: Single-SKU rule confirmed from official datasheet.

statement: SKU 604411 is confirmed as PEHA COMPACTA device_box.
notes: Single-SKU rule confirmed from official datasheet.

statement: SKU 190511 is confirmed as PEHA GENERIC switch.
notes: Single-SKU rule confirmed from official datasheet.

statement: SKU 193111 is confirmed as PEHA GENERIC pushbutton.
notes: Single-SKU rule confirmed from official datasheet.

statement: SKU 210213 is confirmed as PEHA GENERIC dimmer.
notes: Single-SKU rule confirmed from official datasheet.

statement: SKU 902591 is confirmed as PEHA GENERIC speaker_socket.
notes: Single-SKU rule confirmed from official datasheet.

statement: Official Honeywell/PEHA datasheets are a strong trusted source for PEHA family and product-type confirmation.
notes: Use as primary confirmation source when exact PN datasheet exists.

statement: Exact B2B/product pages are usable for PEHA family/type confirmation only when the page confirms the exact PN and product class.
notes: Usable only with exact PN/product confirmation; do not treat generic category pages as equivalent evidence.

statement: Exact product pages outside official manufacturer domains are usable for PEHA family/type confirmation only when exact PN and product context are both explicit.
notes: Useful fallback when official datasheet is missing; still weaker than official manufacturer evidence.

statement: Exact PDF mirrors and catalog mirrors are usable for PEHA family/type confirmation only with exact article-number or PN confirmation; they are weaker than official datasheets.
notes: Validated in this batch via exact PN mapping on AudioPoint and Elektrobode catalog mirrors; never use mirror PDFs without exact code confirmation.

statement: Run-scoped PEHA batch outcome: target 20 SKU resolved to 20 accept / 0 review / 0 fallback; current PEHA slice resolved to 90 accept / 2 review / 2 fallback; unresolved leftovers are 42511 and 57511; pytest tests/enrichment -q passed with 387 tests.
notes: Treat as run-scoped outcome, not universal permanent truth; unresolved leftovers remain review-only and should not be auto-generalized.

statement: Onboard a new brand by finding 1-2 anchor SKUs with strong repo or source clues, deriving family, series, and type coding patterns from those anchors, extending the pattern to the cluster, using external lookup only for unknown clusters, using individual lookup only for edge cases, and then transferring stable patterns into naming rules while recording source findings in the experience layer.
notes: Bootstrap methodology captured from the PEHA batch; intended as reusable brand-onboarding playbook, not runtime logic.

statement: Run-scoped PEHA outcome snapshot: target review subset achieved 20 accept / 0 review / 0 fallback; broader PEHA slice snapshot was 90 accept / 2 review / 2 fallback with unresolved leftovers 42511 and 57511; pytest result was 387 passed.
notes: Treat this as run-scoped outcome only, not universal truth; later PEHA iterations may differ from this snapshot.
